programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: While on the stand in his own double murder trial, Ali Abulaban's attorneys had him explain the marital drama that was filled with physical and verbal abuse. For the first time, jurors heard a different side to his wife's character and behavior in the months leading up to her death. On the stand, Ali painted Ana as more of the villain in the story as a manipulative, demanding, messy and vindictive wife who continued to upset and embarrass him.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Verbal insults and physical violence were heard on an audio recording. This was during the Defense's case where they aimed to show a different side to Ana Abulaban in the months before she and her friend, Rayburn Barron, were killed. The defendant was on the witness stand testifying on how bad things had gotten between him and his wife which ended in a brutal double homicide.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors learned about what happened during the moment Ali Abulaban and his wife, Ana, separated. An alleged extramarital affair and the filing of a restraining order occurred in the window of time just days before it all ended in the double murder. According to Ali Abulaban, it was he who was the victim of verbal abuse, insults, and rejection by his estranged wife and not the other way around.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: The intensity of a marriage separation by both sides was highlighted to jurors. They heard everything from cocaine binges, domestic abuse, Internet searches of chopped up bodies, and extramarital affairs. In the days preceding the murder, Ali Abulaban admitted that he had become increasingly jealous and paranoid about Ana cheating on him. He was also high on cocaine while posting numerous clips of his marital drama on social media.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: On the stand in his own murder trial, Ali Abulaban detailed the events just hours before he committed the double murder. He recalled that he had not slept in over 24 hours and was high on cocaine. Ali elaborated for jurors how heated passion from a cocaine-binge paranoia led him to fatally gun down his wife, Ana, and her friend, Rayburn Barron. This was contrary to the State's theory on what happened. They claimed he premeditated the fatal attack.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: The Moment of the Fatal Shootings
programme.desc: In his murder trial, Ali Abulaban walked the jury through the very moment of the murders.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: From Military Discharge To Behavioral Counseling
programme.desc: Cross-examination continued on the defendant who was trying to appeal to jurors that he's no mastermind killer. Prosecutors challenged Ali Abulaban's credibility in his actions leading up to the shooting deaths of his wife, Ana, and her friend, Rayburn Barron. The lead prosecutor had Ali walk the jury again through his troubled relationship with Ana back when they first dated overseas.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Prosecutors questioned the credibility of the defendant on the witness stand. In the months before the double murders, Ali claimed he was a victim of emotional manipulation and sexual confusion which prompted him to kill out of heated passion. The State alluded to a double standard Ali had for his marriage. According to the defendant when he was separated from his wife, Ana, he cheated on her with another woman but grew upset when Ana was friendly with a male friend.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: The defendant, Ali Abulaban, told jurors there were a million reasons his wife did not want to be with him days before she and her friend, Rayburn Barron, were killed. They included: Ali cheating on her with another woman in their home, the physical abuse, and his growing cocaine addiction. Jurors heard the allegations that Ana was the emotional manipulator, the neglectful wife, and the verbal abuser, according to her estranged husband.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: Defendant Lashes Out On The Stand
programme.desc: While on the stand in his own murder trial, the State portrayed Ali Abulaban as a controlling, abusive husband whose jealousy led him to plot and carry out the murders of his wife, Ana, and her friend, Rayburn Barron. Despite Ana telling him she didn't want to be with him anymore, and she was getting ready to file a restraining order, Ali testified that his marriage was not over. Jurors watched the defendant throw a fit at the lead prosecutor's probing questions.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: Seeking The Truth With A Loaded Gun
programme.desc: Ali Abulaban recalled the very moments leading up to the fatal shootings of his wife, Ana, and her friend, Rayburn Barron. While the State contended that he premeditated both homicides, Ali claimed killing two people was never on his mind. He just wanted to find out the truth about his estranged wife allegedly cheating on him. When he aimed and fired at close range on the two victims, Ali recalled having an out-of-body experience and didn't even remember shooting his own wife.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Crime scene tampering is part of the State's case against Ali Abulaban for double murder.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: While testifying on the stand, Ali Abulaban's defense attorney blew the whistle during his cross-examination. The State brought up a jailhouse confession Ali allegedly made where he bragged about killing his wife, Ana, and her friend, Rayburn Barron. Later, the jury listened to the defendant be apologetic about his role in the fatal shootings. Ali admitted that he was initially in the victim mindset until he began his so-called Journey of healing.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Ali Abulaban's attorney had more questions for him during redirect examination.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1987, the body of Darlene Krashoc is discovered behind a Colorado Springs restaurant. She had been savagely beaten, raped and strangled. Investigators struggle to identify this barbaric killer. 30 years later, science provides a breakthrough.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A town in Washington lives in fear after a 17-year-old girl is abducted and raped without a suspect in sight.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: The 1959 rape and murder of 9-year-old Candy Rogers in Spokane, WA is solved six decades later thanks to DNA.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Police are stumped when a toddler's body is found in Escatawpa River, leading to a decades-long mystery.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Michelle Wyatt is found strangled to death in 1980. Few leads arise until 41 years later, thanks to forensics.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: The NorCal Rapist terrorizes the women of North California for years until forensic science can identify him.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: The murder of Carol Sue Klaber is cold for decades until new technology can finally bring her killer to light.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1987 New Mexico, the body of 38-year-old Donna Hyatt is discovered by her daughter. Two empty glasses of cola suggest she had company the night before. It will take another 36 years before investigative genetic genealogy solves this case.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 2003, a man walking in the Sierra Nevada mountains finds a human skull. In nearby Mammoth Lakes, a family is reeling after a young mother mysteriously skips town. It'll take revolutionary science to piece this puzzle together - 20 years later.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: The identity of Nancy Bennallack's murderer is a mystery until the advent of investigative genetic genealogy.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A Jane Doe found by the side of the I-59 is unidentified until forensic science provides answers years later.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Huntington Beach, 1968. Boys on their way to school discover the brutalized body of a young woman in an oil field. Police are unable to identify her or her killer. It will take 52 years before DNA science is able to solve this double mystery.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1964 Hazleton, Pennsylvania, the brutalized body of 9-year-old Marise Chiverella is discovered after she disappears walking to school. Half a century later, a teen prodigy in investigative genetic genealogy helps detectives solve this crime.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Police work for decades with emerging forensic sciences to identify the killer of two women in Lake Tahoe.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: 40 years after the sexual assault and murder of 20-year-old Robin Brooks in Sacramento, California, determined cold case detectives collaborate with scientists using revolutionary forensic techniques to identify her killer and put him behind bars.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1992, the decapitated body of a young woman is discovered near the Arizona Canal in Phoenix. Ten months later, another body - this time a teen - is located nearby. Dedicated detectives use forensic science to catch the infamous killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Investigative genetic genealogy is used to resolve the 1988 murder of a mother killed in front of her toddler.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Investigative genetic genealogy reveals the killer of a young mother murdered in her own home 34 years prior.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A young woman is found beaten, raped and strangled in Garden Grove, California in 1987. Two years pass until a woman in a neighboring jurisdiction meets the same fate. It will take years before forensics and police identify this serial killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1990 Mississippi, a woman is kidnapped and raped at knifepoint. Police are unable to identify the perpetrator. It'll take forward-thinking investigators, state-of-the-art science and brave survivors to put this man behind bars.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1972 Washington, 20-year-old Jody Loomis is discovered covered in blood by the roadside. She is brought to the hospital where she passes away from a bullet wound. It'll take over forty years for science to reveal her killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Chicago police and the FBI began to suspect that a law enforcement officer was responsible for the crimes.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a rash of home invasions plagued the Detroit area. Each time, masked men disguised as police officers broke in with assault rifles and stole money and jewelry. But when one homeowner shot back, the identity of the intruders was revealed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the mid-1980s, as drug profits grew, gangs ruled the streets of Chicago. Led by mastermind Jeff Fort, the dangerous, power-hungry El Rukn street gang attempted to conspire with Libya to perform acts of domestic terrorism for money.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: An escapee from Castro's Cuba was the mastermind behind a secret terrorist organization called Omega 7.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the upscale town of Lodi, California, a 12-year-old girl was taken from her home. When the kidnapper's car was discovered in a field near the town, the FBI and local police teamed up with hundreds of volunteers in their search for the girl.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, two men used a sledgehammer to break into a Camden, New Jersey home. Police tracked the suspects to a nearby park but the criminals were able to escape after a nearly fatal shootout.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A Puerto Rican separatist group known as the FALN was suspected of setting off bombs in major U.S. cities.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Washington DC, several officers had been sitting in their patrol cars when a man opened fire on them.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1995, a mother of three was murdered and her corpse burned in Los Angeles, California. Agents hunted down a charismatic man named Glen Rogers who used charm and generosity to target women for his brutal attacks.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the 1970s, a Croatian terrorist group committed acts against non-supportive Croatians in the United States.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1992, a young mother was gunned down in front of her children and police were left baffled. The crime seemed unmotivated and random. But family members hinted that the victim, who had been married to a prominent attorney, harbored a dark secret.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.sub-title: The First and Kennedy Street Crew

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1993, an elderly man was found suffocated in his home with his checkbook, credit cards and vehicle stolen.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1985, a Lebanese terrorist and his four accomplices hijacked Royal Jordanian Flight 405 before taking off.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The bodies of several young women began turning up in shallow graves in the Alaskan wilderness. In one of the first cases of successful criminal investigative analysis, FBI profilers mapped the criminal's mind and captured the serial killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1990s Philadelphia, a ruthless grab for power led to a full-scale mob war. Using informants and high-tech surveillance, the FBI infiltrated the most powerful mob family in the city and sought to bring down the organization.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1993, a mother, her 8-year-old son and a live-in nurse were brutally murdered by an assassin. The murder-for-hire plot was meticulously planned and executed.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the killer who took four lives during a chilling two-day killing spree in 2004 after being inspired by horror films.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Dayton Leroy Rogers, known as The Molalla Forest Killer, mutilated and killed at least eight women in the US.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Mary's murder haunted Glasgow for 36 years with no witnesses or leads until advanced forensic techniques finally revealed Graham as the killer, ending decades of uncertainty for her family and investigators.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of a terrifying killer who snatched away the lives of four victims before being caught while trying to claim his fifth.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A dangerous sexual predator murders a young woman, just days after his release from prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The horrifying story of a computer engineer who lured a 14-year-old school boy to his flat and killed him.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Catherine Nevin and her husband Tom were successful publicans and property developers in the Dublin and Wicklow areas. Catherine reported a break-in and violent attack at their pub one night in 1996, which left Tom dead.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: William Bonin is thought to have raped, tortured and murdered a minimum of twenty-one boys and young men.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A family's worst nightmare when 16-year-old Becky Watts goes missing from her home and is found dead a few days' later, murdered by her own stepbrother.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A fugitive from the United States tackles the British Police and goes on the run.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of the serial killer who worked as a nurse in Texas, where she killed many children and infants by injecting them with drugs.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Years of unconnected home murders finally came together when cold-case detectives saw the pattern. They discovered that Emanuel, who moved far away, continued killing with the same familiar method.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Nothing was going to stop Ricky Dubose from breaking free of prison. Whilst being transported on a bus between facilities, he killed two correctional officers before escaping - and going on the run.

programme.desc: Violent armed robber Michael Dutcher brutally murdered two people while attempting a prison escape. He remains a threat to anybody who stands in his way.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Born into a notorious crime family, robber-turned-killer Erica Jenkins goes on a murder spree in Omaha.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Willing to kill for just $20 and drugs, Joshua Gardner commits his first murder at 24. Even behind bars, he can't contain his rage, brutally beating a fellow inmate to death.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners

programme.desc: After shooting his victims in the head, the killer staged the scene, placed the incriminating evidence into a plastic bag and tossed it into the river.

programme.desc: In a tragic twist of fate, just days after the woman sold her home and moved to a modest trailer, a fire took both the trailer and her life. But the autopsy proved this was no accident.

programme.desc: A security guard disappeared from his post without a trace; his remains were found a year later in a remote camp site.

programme.desc: In 2006, Texas real estate agent Sarah Anne Walker was found brutally murdered in a model home.

programme.desc: In 2003, St. Cloud teenager Jason MacLennan returned home after a night out with friends and discovered his father Ken's body lying in a pool of blood.

programme.desc: The victim has been stabbed more than thirty times, and the crime scene is awash with her blood. Near her head, police discover a distinctive button with strands of thread still attached.
